content: Pangenotypic DAA regimens (preferred, genotype testing not required): glecaprevir/pibrentasvir 3 tabs daily x8 weeks (treatment-naïve without cirrhosis) or x12 weeks (compensated cirrhosis); sofosbuvir/velpatasvir 1 tab daily x12 weeks for all genotypes. Decompensated cirrhosis (Child-Pugh B/C): sofosbuvir/velpatasvir + ribavirin x12 weeks or sofosbuvir/velpatasvir x24 weeks (protease inhibitors contraindicated). Prior treatment failure: tailored retreatment based on prior regimen, resistance-associated substitutions. HIV/HCV coinfection: same regimens as HCV monotherapy; check drug-drug interactions with ART. Post-SVR: monitor for HCC if cirrhosis present (SVR reduces but doesn't eliminate risk). No role for interferon-based therapy. Universal HCV screening recommended for adults 18-79 years.
